mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate below its trailing 12-month average signals continued monetary accommodation, lowering the equity discount rate and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.56 percent, well below historical tightening thresholds
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or accommodative FEDFUNDS periods show no positive return skew relative to base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window (Sahm-rule-like trigger) signals an emerging labor market downturn that precedes broad economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5 percent near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a triggered UNRATE breach are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth and retail sales growth move in the same positive direction simultaneously, the joint confirmation of supply-side output expansion and demand-side consumption strength signals a synchronized economic upswing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; divergence between them nullifies the signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.55 and RSAFS at 683974 million dollars in latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint positive co-movement of INDPRO and RSAFS month-over-month fails to produce SPX 20-day forward returns above the unconditional mean in a 3-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months), a decline in housing starts signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that leads broader economic activity lower, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In easing or flat-rate regimes the same HOUST decline carries no predictive power because it reflects supply-side normalization rather than demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months and HOUST declining over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening regimes are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns in a 3-year rolling walk-forward sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 conditional on the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in headline CPI signals rising inflationary pressure that raises near-term rate-hike expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL month-over-month acceleration and 5-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the policy stance becomes restrictive enough to meaningfully raise borrowing costs across the economy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until the rate falls back below that threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 2.56 percent, below the restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent are not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) in historical walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously ticks up, the joint deterioration signals an emerging labor market inflection that markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. Payroll deceleration alone without a UNRATE increase reflects noise rather than a genuine turning point.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153018 thousand persons and UNRATE at 3.5 percent
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE increase fail to produce negative 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of historical co-occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by low unemployment and elevated rates, a deceleration in real GDP growth signals waning momentum that precedes an equity market correction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In early or mid-cycle regimes the same GDP deceleration is a normal pause and carries no predictive signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate declines for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19895.271 billion chained dollars, UNRATE at 3.5 percent indicating late-cycle labor tightness
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration during low-UNRATE late-cycle regimes is not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns across at least two historical late-cycle episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03.
